intent.feature_concept: Working-tabs state machine + request data model (RequestSpec) for an HTTP client, built as a zustand tabs slice with a TabBar strip UI
intent.topic: Working-tabs state machine + request data model (RequestSpec) for an HTTP client, built as a zustand tabs slice with a TabBar strip UI
intent.scope_summary: A zustand tabs slice owning the open-request tabs + the lifecycle state machine (open-from-collection with dedupe id-then-url, new-blank, close never-zero, select-active), a per-tab dirty flag + markClean(tabId) action, and new-tab seed defaults. The RequestSpec domain model: { method, url, name, params[], headers[], body:{lang,type,text}, auth } where each param/header row is { enabled, key, value, description } and auth is a discriminated union on type ({none}+{bearer,token} in scope). A TabBar strip UI composing the existing Tabs primitive (extended with close ✕, '+' new-tab, dirty marker) and wiring it to the slice. Out of scope: request-editing panels, real send/save, variable interpolation, other auth types, auth→wire-header derivation.
intent.verbatim_prompt: The working-tabs state machine and request data model for an HTTP client. Each open tab holds a RequestSpec: { method, url, name, params[], headers[], body: {lang, type, text}, auth }, where each param/header row is { enabled, key, value, description }. auth is a discriminated union keyed on `type`; T4 defines ONLY the shape and the two variants it actually needs — { type: 'none' } and { type: 'bearer', token } — and leaves auth intentionally extensible: the full set of auth types and their per-type fields (basic user/pass, api-key with name+in, oauth2, etc.) is owned by the auth-panel task and is out of scope here. auth is the SINGLE source of truth for authorization — do NOT also seed an Authorization row into headers[]; turning auth into an actual wire header is the http-engine's job, not duplicated tab state. Tab lifecycle: open-from-collection (dedupe by id then url), new blank tab, close (never leave zero tabs — closing the last spawns a fresh Untitled GET), select-active. A dirty flag flips on any edit; T4 also exposes a markClean(tabId) action but does NOT own its triggers — a successful send (http-engine task) or save (persistence task) calls markClean, both out of scope here, so what T4 implements and tests is the flag plus the action, never a real send. New tabs seed auth = { type: 'bearer', token: '{{apiKey}}' } (the literal {{apiKey}} string is stored verbatim; variable interpolation is a separate task) and headers[] with a default Accept row. Implemented as a zustand tabs slice with a TabBar strip as UI; the request-editing panels are out of scope. Visual reference for the TabBar: design/reference.html (a Claude Design export) — use it for look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup; it carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must not enter the build. Rebuild with semantic class names from role and tag function. Colors come from design/tokens.json.

constraints.content: auth is the SINGLE source of truth for authorization — NEVER seed an Authorization row into headers[] (deriving the wire header is the http-engine's job). auth shape = discriminated union on `type`; T4 defines ONLY {type:'none'} and {type:'bearer',token}, intentionally extensible (other variants owned by auth-panel). New tabs seed auth={type:'bearer',token:'{{apiKey}}'} with literal {{apiKey}} stored verbatim (no interpolation) + a default Accept header row. Never zero tabs. Import/layer boundaries: lib/ slice is renderer-only (no node/electron imports), must NOT import components/, collection store must NOT be imported; RequestSpec stays framework-agnostic in the domain layer; constitution §2.1/§2.3/§3.3/§4 apply. TabBar uses semantic class names from role/tag function; NONE of design/reference.html's generated cruft (data-om-* attrs, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) enters the build; colors strictly from design/tokens.json.

Out of scope: OUT of scope: request-editing panels (params/headers/body/auth editors); real send (http-engine task) and real save (persistence task), plus their markClean trigger sites — T4 ships only the markClean operation, never a real send; bearer-token variable interpolation (the seeded {{ }} literal is stored verbatim here); auth variants beyond {none}+{bearer} and their per-type fields (basic, api-key, oauth2 — owned by auth-panel task); deriving the Authorization wire header from auth (http-engine's job); the collection store itself (T4 consumes a RequestSpec payload, never imports it); reproducing design/reference.html markup or any of its generated cruft. NOTE: RequestSpec (de)serialization shape IS in scope per the I/O dimension — not deferred.

affected_areas.impact: existing capability — candidate for reuse over fresh build; TabBar composes it; its {id,label,badge?,disabled?} selection-only contract is EXTENDED (close ✕ sibling button, '+' control, dirty marker) — record extension in /specify
existing capability — new tabsStore.ts mirrors this slice convention; reuse over inventing a new state pattern
existing capability — TabBar mounts into the existing 'tabs' slot above PaneSplit; no new layout plumbing
internal — existing tab a11y/roving-tabindex + selection primitive (002); TabBar composes & extends it (close ✕, '+', dirty), does NOT re-roll
internal — module-level create<State>((set)=>...), Store suffix (§3.3), renderer-only, selectors; tabsStore mirrors this exactly
internal — existing mount point above PaneSplit where TabBar renders; no new layout plumbing needed

risks.risk: Spike the Tabs-primitive close-✕ extension (close button as sibling to role=tab + APG Delete key) against the primitives existing roving-tabindex tests, confirming the contract extension is non-breaking BEFORE building the TabBar
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Pin the RequestSpec serialization JSON shape as a shared contract test with the out-of-scope persistence task before implementing, to avoid rework when persistence lands
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Establish the renderer test stack (Vitest + Testing Library per constitution §3.4 — no test infra exists yet) as part of this feature, since the success bar requires slice unit tests
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Decide and fixture the url-dedupe normalization rule (trailing slash, case) and the dirty-tab-close behavior (warn/confirm vs silent-drop) during /specify
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: per-tab close button must not nest inside role=tab (would break roving tabindex)
needs APG Delete-key handler on the focused tab

open_questions.question: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): implement as a zustand tabs slice + TabBar strip UI, request-editing panels out of scope

design_options.name: Flat tab array with embedded spec
design_options.shape: state = { tabs: Tab[]; activeTabId: string } where Tab = { id; spec: RequestSpec; dirty }. Tab order IS the array order. Actions find-by-id over a small N; serialization = tabs.map(t => t.spec).
pros: Array order naturally encodes tab order; no separate order list to sync
Trivial (de)serialization to the persistence JSON shape
Close-neighbor selection is a simple index walk
cons: O(n) find-by-id on markClean/updateActive (irrelevant for small N tab counts)

design_options.name: Normalized id-keyed map plus order list
design_options.shape: state = { tabsById: Record<string,Tab>; order: string[]; activeTabId }. Lookups and updates are by id; order[] holds tab sequence separately.
pros: O(1) lookup/update by id for markClean, updateActive, dedupe-by-id
cons: Two structures (order vs tabsById) must be kept in sync on every open/close
More boilerplate than the array for no real gain at small N
dedupe-by-url is still an O(n) scan

design_options.name: Spec registry with tab refs (decoupled)
design_options.shape: RequestSpecs live in their own registry map; tabs hold only spec-id refs, so one spec could back multiple tabs/views.
pros: Decouples spec lifetime from tab lifetime
Enables future multi-view of a single spec
cons: Over-engineered for an in-memory single-window scope (YAGNI)
No requirement in scope motivates the decoupling

build_vs_buy.build_path: Build the tabsStore slice + the RequestSpec domain model fresh, composing the existing internal Tabs primitive and the zustand dependency the project already ships. No new runtime dependency.
build_vs_buy.buy_path: Adopt an external request-collection model/SDK (e.g. postman-collection, or Bruno's bru parser) to supply the RequestSpec shape and (de)serialization.
build_vs_buy.reasoning: The buy path drags a heavyweight collection SDK in to model a handful of in-memory fields — a poor trade for an extensible discriminated-union we fully control, and it would couple the domain entity to a third-party schema. zustand is already a dependency and the Tabs primitive already exists, so the build path is mostly composition of internal assets; only the domain model + state machine are genuinely net-new. Bruno/OpenCollection inform the shape as a reference, not as a runtime dependency.

cited_canonical_patterns.relevance: internal — existing tab a11y/roving-tabindex + selection primitive (002); TabBar composes & extends it (close ✕, '+', dirty), does NOT re-roll
internal — module-level create<State>((set)=>...), Store suffix (§3.3), renderer-only, selectors; tabsStore mirrors this exactly
internal — existing mount point above PaneSplit where TabBar renders; no new layout plumbing needed

GAP: RequestSpec shape — validates {method,url,headers,params(typed array),body{type,data},auth{type,...}}; auth types none/inherit/basic/bearer/apikey/digest/oauth2/awsv4/ntlm confirm the extensible discriminated-union with none+bearer as the minimal seed
GAP: tab state machine — canonical single-slice shape (tabs[] + activeTab + actions); matches the project's existing module-level store convention
GAP: per-tab dirty/unsaved tracking — validates a tabs slice holding per-tab document state + dirty flag, markClean on save/send

plan_seeds.recommended_option_rationale: Extend the existing store convention rather than invent a new one: tabsStore mirrors internal:src/renderer/src/lib/settingsStore.ts (module-level create<State>, Store suffix, renderer-only) and the TabBar composes internal:src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Tabs.tsx instead of re-rolling tab a11y. The flat array is the KISS choice — at realistic tab counts the O(n) find-by-id is a non-issue, array order encodes tab order for free, and serialization to the persistence JSON shape is a trivial map. The normalized id-keyed map buys O(1) updates the scope does not need while adding an order/tabsById sync burden; the spec-registry option solves a multi-view problem no requirement raises (YAGNI). Acknowledged tradeoff: close→select-neighbor needs explicit index bookkeeping, and updateActive/markClean do an array find — both cheap and well-contained.

discovery_block.fit_rationale: Fit is Good and effort Low. All three touchpoints already exist and match: the zustand slice drops straight into the settingsStore/toastStore convention, the TabBar mounts into Shell's existing 'tabs' slot, and the Tabs primitive is reused rather than re-rolled. The only real net-new work is the GAP capabilities with no internal prior art — the RequestSpec domain model and the tab state machine (lifecycle + dirty/markClean). The one belief-vs-reality correction is favorable: the user expected to extend the Tabs primitive with both a '+' control and per-tab close ✕, but the '+' fits the primitive's existing actions? slot (no contract change) — only the per-tab close ✕ (sibling-to-role=tab + APG Delete) is a genuine contract extension, which /specify must record explicitly.

fit_assessments.user_expected: Extend the primitive contract with per-tab close ✕, a '+' new-tab control, and a dirty marker
fit_assessments.reality: Primitive is controlled/selection-only (tabs[], activeId, onChange) and ALREADY exposes an actions? slot rendered OUTSIDE role=tablist (AC-8) — the '+' new-tab control fits there with NO contract change. Only per-tab close ✕ needs a real extension: a close button rendered as a SIBLING to the role=tab label (never nested) + an APG Delete-key path. Dirty marker can ride the existing badge? slot. Net: extension is narrower than the user assumed.
blockers: per-tab close button must not nest inside role=tab (would break roving tabindex)
needs APG Delete-key handler on the focused tab

fit_assessments.reality: Exact match: module-level create<State>((set)=>...), Store suffix (§3.3), renderer-only (no node/electron imports), selector usage. No new state pattern invented; tabsStore drops straight into the convention.

fit_assessments.user_expected: TabBar mounts into Shell's existing tabs slot
fit_assessments.reality: Confirmed: Shell renders a 'tabs' slot above PaneSplit, currently fed only by a test fixture (ShellWithTabsFixture). Wiring the real TabBar is a slot fill — no new layout plumbing.

functional_scope.value: A zustand tabs slice owning the open-request tabs + the lifecycle state machine (open-from-collection with dedupe id-then-url, new-blank, close never-zero, select-active), a per-tab dirty flag + markClean(tabId) action, and new-tab seed defaults. The RequestSpec domain model: { method, url, name, params[], headers[], body:{lang,type,text}, auth } where each param/header row is { enabled, key, value, description } and auth is a discriminated union on type ({none}+{bearer,token} in scope). A TabBar strip UI composing the existing Tabs primitive (extended with close ✕, '+' new-tab, dirty marker) and wiring it to the slice. Out of scope: request-editing panels, real send/save, variable interpolation, other auth types, auth→wire-header derivation.

users.value: Two consumer classes. (1) End user: operates the TabBar — open/close/switch request tabs. (2) Sibling tasks consuming the zustand slice programmatically: collection-list opens tabs (open-from-collection), http-engine reads active RequestSpec + calls markClean on successful send, persistence calls markClean on save, request-editing panels bind to the active tab's RequestSpec. T4 owns only the slice + TabBar + the markClean action; the trigger sites live in those sibling tasks (out of scope).

inputs_outputs.value: IN: a RequestSpec payload (from the collection store) for open-from-collection; per-field edits from editing panels via an updateActive(patch) action. OUT (selectors): tabs[] (id + RequestSpec + dirty), activeTabId, the active RequestSpec. OUT (actions): openFromCollection, newTab, closeTab, selectTab, updateActive(patch), markClean(tabId). The slice/domain ALSO owns RequestSpec (de)serialization to/from the persistence JSON shape (RequestSpec is a framework-agnostic serializable domain entity) — but NOT the actual disk read/write nor the markClean trigger, both owned by the persistence task. In-memory at the slice level — no network, no disk I/O here.

integration_points.value: tabsStore in src/renderer/src/lib/ (mirrors settingsStore.ts / toastStore.ts; module-level create<State>, Store suffix, renderer-only — lib/ must NOT import components/). RequestSpec is a framework-agnostic serializable domain entity in the domain layer (not the store). TabBar COMPOSES the existing components/molecules/Tabs.tsx primitive (002) — do NOT re-roll tab a11y/roving-tabindex — and wires it to the store, mounted into Shell.tsx's existing 'tabs' slot above PaneSplit. This EXTENDS the Tabs primitive contract (currently {id,label,badge?,disabled?}, selection-only) and that extension MUST be recorded explicitly in /specify, not assumed: (a) per-tab trailing close ✕ as a SIBLING button, NOT nested in role=tab (nested interactives break roving tabindex); APG keyboard path = Delete on focused tab; (b) a '+' new-tab control placed AFTER the tablist, outside the roving set; (c) dirty indicator via the existing badge? slot or a parallel marker — decided in /specify. open-from-collection is a store action taking a RequestSpec payload; the collection store is out of scope and must NOT be imported.

constraints.value: auth is the SINGLE source of truth for authorization — NEVER seed an Authorization row into headers[] (deriving the wire header is the http-engine's job). auth shape = discriminated union on `type`; T4 defines ONLY {type:'none'} and {type:'bearer',token}, intentionally extensible (other variants owned by auth-panel). New tabs seed auth={type:'bearer',token:'{{apiKey}}'} with literal {{apiKey}} stored verbatim (no interpolation) + a default Accept header row. Never zero tabs. Import/layer boundaries: lib/ slice is renderer-only (no node/electron imports), must NOT import components/, collection store must NOT be imported; RequestSpec stays framework-agnostic in the domain layer; constitution §2.1/§2.3/§3.3/§4 apply. TabBar uses semantic class names from role/tag function; NONE of design/reference.html's generated cruft (data-om-* attrs, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) enters the build; colors strictly from design/tokens.json.

non_goals.value: OUT of scope: request-editing panels (params/headers/body/auth editors); real send (http-engine task) and real save (persistence task), plus their markClean trigger sites — T4 ships only the markClean operation, never a real send; bearer-token variable interpolation (the seeded {{ }} literal is stored verbatim here); auth variants beyond {none}+{bearer} and their per-type fields (basic, api-key, oauth2 — owned by auth-panel task); deriving the Authorization wire header from auth (http-engine's job); the collection store itself (T4 consumes a RequestSpec payload, never imports it); reproducing design/reference.html markup or any of its generated cruft. NOTE: RequestSpec (de)serialization shape IS in scope per the I/O dimension — not deferred.

success_criteria.value: Slice unit-tested: every lifecycle action (open-from-collection dedupe id-then-url, new-blank, close never-zero, select-active) + dirty flag + markClean(tabId) covered; invariants asserted (never-zero tabs, dedupe, seed defaults = auth bearer {{apiKey}} verbatim + default Accept header, auth NOT mirrored into headers[]). TabBar renders/selects/closes AND visually matches design/reference.html (look/spacing/states) against design/tokens.json. typecheck + lint + build clean.

edge_cases.value: Lifecycle boundaries: close last/only tab → spawn fresh Untitled GET (never zero); close active tab → select a neighbor; open-from-collection dedupe matches by id first then url → focus existing instead of duplicating. Dirty/markClean races: markClean on non-existent/already-closed tabId = safe no-op; editing a clean tab flips dirty=true; markClean idempotent on unmodified tab; closing a dirty tab → warn/confirm vs silent-drop decided in /specify. Seed/identity integrity: every new tab gets a unique id; {{apiKey}} token + Accept header seeded verbatim (no interpolation); empty/blank-url tab valid; url dedupe normalization (trailing slash, case) defined in /specify. Selection integrity: activeTabId always points at an existing tab after any mutation; removing the active tab never leaves a dangling activeTabId.

references: Postman/Insomnia/Bruno/Hoppscotch — request/tab model concepts (Bruno .bru schema MIT, readable; concepts only, not 1:1 copy)
Existing forge zustand stores — settingsStore.ts / toastStore.ts conventions
RequestSpec as framework-agnostic serializable domain entity living in the domain layer

gaps.description: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): implement as a zustand tabs slice + TabBar strip UI, request-editing panels out of scope
